mission.title: Install and Configure Rancher / RKE2
mission.description: Rancher is a complete container management platform that simplifies deploying and managing Kubernetes clusters across various environments. It is ideal for organizations looking to streamline their container orchestration and management processes. Key trade-offs include the need for adequate resources and potential complexity in multi-cluster management.

steps.title: Install the Rancher CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Rancher CLI, which is necessary for managing Rancher from the command line.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/rancher/cli/releases/download/v2.14.1/rancher-darwin-amd64.tar.gz

curl -LO https://github.com/rancher/cli/releases/download/v2.14.1/rancher-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf rancher-*.tar.gz && sudo mv rancher /usr/local/bin/

```

> For macOS users
> For Linux users
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using RKE2 with the specified version and node count.

```bash

curl -sfL https://get.rke2.io | sh -

systemctl enable rke2-server.service && systemctl start rke2-server.service

export KUBECONFIG=/etc/rancher/rke2/rke2.yaml

```

> Install RKE2
> Start the RKE2 server
> Set the kubeconfig for RKE2
commands: systemctl enable rke2-server.service && systemctl start rke2-server.service

steps.title: Verify the cluster nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in the newly created cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes in a 'Ready' state

steps.title: Install Rancher using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs Rancher on the Kubernetes cluster using Helm, allowing for easy management of Kubernetes clusters.

```bash

helm repo add rancher-stable https://charts.rancher.io

helm install rancher rancher-stable/rancher --namespace cattle-system --create-namespace --version 2.14.1

kubectl -n cattle-system rollout status deploy/rancher

```

> Add the Rancher Helm repository
> Install Rancher with the specified version
> Verify that the Rancher deployment is complete

uninstall.title: Uninstall Rancher
uninstall.description:
This step removes Rancher from the cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall rancher --namespace cattle-system

kubectl delete namespace cattle-system

```

> Uninstall Rancher
> Delete the namespace and all resources within it

upgrade.title: Upgrade Rancher
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your Rancher data. This step will upgrade Rancher to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl -n cattle-system get pods -o name | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade rancher rancher-stable/rancher --namespace cattle-system --version 2.14.1

kubectl -n cattle-system rollout status deploy/rancher

```

> Drain the nodes before upgrading
> Upgrade Rancher to the specified version
> Verify that the upgrade was successful
commands: kubectl -n cattle-system get pods -o name | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error occurs when the kubeconfig file is not configured correctly or the server certificate is not trusted.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the KUBECONFIG environment variable points to the correct kubeconfig file and that the server certificate is trusted.

troubleshooting.title: Error: context deadline exceeded
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes API server is not reachable, possibly due to network issues or the server being down.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the RKE2 server and ensure that it is running and accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Error: failed to install CRDs: CustomResourceDefinition.apiextensions.k8s.io "ranchers" already exists
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This happens when trying to install Rancher while CRDs from a previous installation still exist.

**Fix:**
Delete the existing CRDs using 'kubectl delete crd ranchers.rancher.io' before reinstalling.

troubleshooting.title: Error: ImagePullBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Rancher image cannot be pulled from the registry, possibly due to incorrect image name or network issues.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and check network connectivity to the Docker registry.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.30, improved UI for cluster management, and enhanced RBAC features.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.27.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from a version below 2.14.0, ensure to back up your data and follow the upgrade path outlined in the documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with nodes listed as 'Ready'. Rancher should be accessible via the specified URL.
codeSnippets: systemctl enable rke2-server.service && systemctl start rke2-server.service

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
